meta.title: Battery Barn | Advanced Battery Concepts
meta.description: 5 MW / 20 MWh utility-scale energy storage in 6,000 sq ft. No HVAC, no fire suppression, 15-year daily cycle life warranty. ABC Battery Barn.
meta.og_description: 5 MW / 20 MWh utility-scale energy storage in 6,000 sq ft. No HVAC, no fire suppression, 15-year daily cycle life warranty. ABC Battery Barn.

items.title: $0 Augmentation
items.desc: Lithium-ion systems require battery augmentation every 7 to 10 years to maintain rated capacity. ABC's less than 0.1% annual degradation eliminates this entirely. Zero augmentation cost over 20 years.

items.title: $0 HVAC and Fire Suppression
items.desc: No chillers. No heaters. No active fire suppression systems. No annual maintenance contracts for climate control or suppression equipment. These avoided costs compound over the life of the project.

rows.spec: Less than 0.1%/yr, no measurable fade over design life
100%, positive residual value at end of life

cta.label: Get Started
cta.title: Evaluate Battery Barn for Your Project
cta.desc: Our engineering team will assess your site requirements, load profile, and interconnection constraints to determine if a Battery Barn deployment is the right fit.

safety.label: Safety and Compliance
safety.title: No HVAC. No Fire Suppression. No Compromise.

items.title: Non-Flammable Chemistry
items.desc: Aqueous sulfuric acid electrolyte absorbed in AGM separators. No organic solvent. No flammable liquid. Zero fire propagation risk. Eliminates NFPA 855 thermal barrier and explosion venting requirements.

items.title: Zero Thermal Runaway
items.desc: Lead-acid electrochemistry does not support exothermic thermal runaway. The battery cannot self-heat to ignition. Eliminates the catastrophic failure mode that drives prescriptive requirements for lithium-ion installations.

items.title: Standard Construction
items.desc: Deploys in standard commercial warehouse structures. No explosion-rated construction, no fire-rated separation walls, no active suppression systems. Reduces construction cost and accelerates permitting timelines.

items.title: Hydrogen Management
items.desc: Sealed VRLA design with flame arrestors on every vent port. Continuous mechanical ventilation maintains hydrogen below 1.0% by volume, well below the 4.0% lower flammability limit.

architecture.label: System Architecture
architecture.heading: Cartridge Matrix Configuration
architecture.desc: 432 CEES™ cartridges arranged in a 4-high by 6-row by 18-column matrix. Each cartridge contains forty-eight 48V bipolar lead modules delivering 48 kWh with flexible nominal voltage options including 48V, 192V, 288V, and 384V. Cartridges are mechanically fastened to adjacent units with a maximum 4-high stack limit per structural analysis.
list_items: DC System Voltage: 1,152V (4 cartridges in series)
10-foot row spacing for forklift maintenance access
High-voltage DC electrical room physically separated from battery area

Total energy storage capacity from 432 CEES™ cartridges at 48 kWh each
Total building footprint. Standard commercial warehouse construction.
Full rated discharge duration at maximum power output
System voltage. 4 cartridges in series per string, 108 strings in parallel.
Daily cycle life warranty. Engineered for one full deep cycle per day for 15 years.
Capacity fade. No measurable fade over the design life.

hero.label: Utility-Scale Deployment
hero.title: Battery Barn: 5 MW / 20 MWh in 6,000 Square Feet
hero.desc: A purpose-built warehouse facility housing 432 CEES™ cartridges. No HVAC. No fire suppression. No augmentation. Where lithium-ion demands 3 to 5 acres of outdoor space, ABC delivers the same capacity in a single 6,000 square-foot building.

footprint.label: Restrained Land Resources
footprint.title: 20 MWh on 0.2 Acres
footprint.desc: Land is the constraint that kills most utility-scale storage projects. Permitting delays, environmental setbacks, community opposition, and real estate costs all scale with acreage. The ABC Battery Barn eliminates this bottleneck entirely. 20 MWh consolidates into a single 6,000 sq ft commercial building that can be sited directly adjacent to the substation, on existing industrial parcels, or in urban infill locations where 3 to 5 acres of lithium-ion pad space simply does not exist. No fire setbacks. No blast-rated exclusion zones. No HVAC equipment yards. The result is a deployment that fits where others cannot.
footprint.image_alt: Aerial view comparing ABC Battery Barn 0.2 acre footprint versus lithium-ion 3 to 5 acre requirement
